revision.text:
[[ja:御霊神社 (五條市霊安寺町)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Goryō Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:五條市霊安寺町 御霊神社 Goryō-jinja, Ryōanji-chō 2011.3.31 - panoramio.jpg | location = Nara Prefecture, Gojō City, Ryōanji-chō 2206 | ISO = JP-13 | deity = {{ill|Inoe-naishinnō|qid=Q7244649|1=Princess Inoe}} | Sacred Object = Wooden Seated Statue of Goryō Daijin | Architectural Style = Three-bay nagare-zukuri, Momoyama period style | alternate_name = Goryō Hongū, Goryō-no-Miya Daimyōjin, Shisho Goryō Daimyōjin, Uchi-gun Ōmiya, Goryō-san | festival = Fourth Sunday in April (Grand Kagura Festival, held biennially)
October 8 (Seasonal Festival)
Fourth Sunday in October (Main Festival) | location = {{ill|Gojō|qid=Q602571|1=Gojō, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.3355|N|135.69794444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Goryō Shrine''' (御霊神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Gojō City|en|五條市|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō City|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is also known by various names, including Goryō Hongū, Goryō-no-Miya Daimyōjin, and Shisho Goryō Daimyōjin. The shrine is notable for its worship of {{ill|Princess Inoe|en|Princess Inoe|qid=Q7244649|lt=Princess Inoue|1=Princess Inoe}}, among others, and holds the historical status of a prefectural shrine. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four deities:"Goryō Shrine History," p. 1. * Main Shrine: {{ill|Princess Inoe|en|Princess Inoe|qid=Q7244649|lt=Princess Inoue|1=Princess Inoe}} * Southern Side Shrine: {{ill|Prince Ōsabe|ja|他戸親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōsabe|1=Osabe-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11377635}} * Northern Side Shrine: {{ill|Prince Sawara|en|Prince Sawara|qid=Q1079634|lt=Prince Sawara|1=Prince Sawara}} * Separate Shrine: Honoikazuchi-no-Kami (third child of Princess Inoue, distinct from the deity born of Izanagi) == History == Princess Inoue, enshrined at Goryō Shrine, was born in 717 (Yōrō 1) as the daughter of Emperor Shōmu. At the age of five, she served as a {{ill|Saiō|en|斎王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiō|qq=}} at the Ise Grand Shrine. Later, she married {{ill|Prince Shirakabe|ja|白壁王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shirakabe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 光仁天皇|1=Emperor Kōnin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q958662}} (later Emperor Kōnin). In 770 (Hōki 1), Prince Shirakabe ascended to the throne as the 49th Emperor Kōnin, and Princess Inoue became Empress. However, political conflicts led to her exile in 773 (Hōki 4), along with her son, Prince Ōsabe. She passed away in 775 (Hōki 6)."Goryō Shrine History," p. 2. Subsequent natural disasters and epidemics in the capital were attributed to her vengeful spirit. In response, the court restored her posthumous honor, built the Ryōanji Temple to appease her spirit, and founded the Goryō Shrine during the same period. Over time, disputes led to the division of the shrine into ten locations, and further divisions resulted in more than 20 Goryō Shrines throughout the Uchi District (modern-day Gojō City). === Key Historical Events === * 717 (Yōrō 1): Birth of Princess Inoue. * 761 (Tenpyō Hōji 5): Birth of Prince Ōsabe. * 770 (Hōki 1): Prince Shirakabe ascends as Emperor Kōnin. * 775 (Hōki 6): Death of Princess Inoue and Prince Ōsabe in exile. * 800 (Enryaku 19): Posthumous restoration of Princess Inoue as Empress and construction of Ryōanji Temple. == Festivals and Events == * Fourth Sunday in April: Grand Kagura Festival (held biennially) * October 8: Seasonal Festival (features a ritual where the young deity Honoikazuchi-no-Kami meets the main deity, Princess Inoue) * Fourth Sunday in October: Main Festival (includes portable shrine processions and other traditional events) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Daihannya-kyō|en|大般若経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihannya-kyō|qq=}}, 530 volumes, stored at Mangansan Temple. === Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Main Shrine: Three-bay nagare-zukuri style, constructed in 1637 (Kan'ei 14). * Wooden Seated Statue of Goryō Daijin: Late Heian period. == Access == * From {{ill|JR Wakayama Line|en|和歌山線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Wakayama Line|qq=}} {{ill|Gojō Station (Nara)|en|五条駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Station (Nara)|qq=}}, take a bus towards Kido or Totsukawa and alight at Ryōanji stop, then walk approximately 3 minutes. * Approximately 30 minutes on foot or 5 minutes by taxi from JR Wakayama Line Gojō Station. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://goryojinja.or.jp/ Official Website of Goryō Shrine] * {{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/137108/20150320-0002.pdf Overview of Nara Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties (2014)]}} {{coord|34|20|7.8|N|135|41|52.6|E|region:JP|display=title}} {{translated page|ja|御霊神社 (五條市霊安寺町)|version=81014568|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:御霊神社 (五條市霊安寺町)]] [[ja:御霊神社]] {{wikidata link|Q30924976}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Gojo]] [[Category:Culture of Gojo]] [[Category:Goryō faith]] [[Category:History of Gojo, Nara]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
